Liquidity Jumps to 9.7% as Government Flows Surge, Credit Holds at 8.9%
body: Indonesia’s liquidity accelerates to 9.7% in March, driven by government flows, while credit growth holds steady at 8.9%.

title: UK’s Babcock Helps Indonesia Build 1,582 Fishing Ships
body: Babcock says "getting the design right" is very important to adjust to Indonesian waters.

title: Stocks Slide in Jakarta as US-Iran Deadlock Fuels Global Uncertainty
body: Global tensions push crude higher, triggering caution across equity markets.

title: Indonesia’s Rice Stockpile Reaches Record 5 Million Tons, Highest in History
body: Government data showed a positive production trend, with national rice output in 2025 rising 13.29% year-on-year to 34.69 million tons.

title: Businesses Adapt as Indonesia Pushes De-Dollarization
body: Business groups say de-dollarization should be seen as diversification rather than a fundamental shift away from the US dollar.

title: JCI Sinks as Iran Deadlock Meets Rising Fuel Burden at Home
body: Jakarta stocks drop on global conflict risks and weakening purchasing power, with heavy selling across sectors.

title: Astra Sets $908 M Dividend, Appoints New CEO
body: Astra Intl approved a $908 M dividend payout and reshuffled its board, appointing a new president director and commissioner lineup.

title: BI Intervenes as Rupiah Weakens Beyond 17,300 to Historic Low
body: Rupiah falls past 17,300 per dollar to a record low as global tensions rise, prompting Bank Indonesia to intensify market intervention.

title: Pertamina Locks In 80,000 KL Fuel to Back 2026 Hajj Travel
body: Pertamina prepares over 80,000 kl of aviation fuel to ensure smooth 2026 hajj flights, with supply secured across 14 embarkation airports.

title: BI Boosts Bonds, Raises Yields to Support Rupiah
body: Bank Indonesia boosts bond purchases and raises yields to attract inflows and support rupiah stability.

title: JCI Climbs on Thursday as Wall Street Rally Offsets Iran Tensions
body: JCI rose 0.30% at the open as markets shifted to earnings, even as Iran tensions and energy supply disruptions remained unresolved.

title: Rupiah Still Undervalued, Bank Indonesia Governor Says
body: As of April 21, 2026, the rupiah stood at Rp 17,140 per US dollar, weakening 0.87% point-to-point from the end of March.

title: Indonesia Expands Waste-to-Power Push With Five New Plants
body: The strategic projects are expected to process more than 7,000 tons of waste per day.

title: De-Dollarization Drive: Indonesia-China Cross-Border QRIS Payments to Launch Next Week
body: Indonesia and China will launch cross-border QRIS payments on April 30 as Jakarta pushes de-dollarization.

title: Rupiah Set to Stay Fragile Despite BI Hold Amid US–Iran Uncertainty
body: Rupiah weakens to Rp 17,181 and is seen drifting toward Rp 17,220 as Hormuz risks and Fed signals outweigh BI’s steady rate stance.

title: Bank Indonesia Holds Rate at 4.75% for Seventh Straight Month
body: BI held rates at 4.75% for a seventh month, prioritizing rupiah stability as global risks and capital outflow pressures persist.

title: Private-Sector Bloc Targets $9.5 Trillion Halal Market by 2030
body: B57+ targets the $9.5 trillion halal market by 2030, aiming to boost trade and investment.

title: JCI Remains Stuck in the Red on Iran, MSCI Concerns
body: JCI closed at 7,541 as stalled US–Iran talks and MSCI’s ongoing review kept investors cautious despite Bank Indonesia holding rates.

title: IDX Lifts Lid on HSC Stocks as MSCI Risk Hits BREN, DSSA
body: IDX has detailed how it flags stocks with concentrated ownership as MSCI keeps restrictions in place, with BREN and DSSA sliding sharply.

title: Imported Australian Cattle Prices Jump 40% Above Cap
body: Australian cattle prices now exceed Indonesia’s cap by up to Rp25,000/kg, raising risks of beef price hikes in Greater Jakarta.
